title: [TASK] Implement BaseLayout, Header, and Footer components with i18n and __root.tsx integration

conversation_log.summary: Task is architecturally sound and consistent with the planned architecture. File paths (apps/frontend/src/components/layout/*.tsx) follow the PascalCase component file + kebab-case folder conventions. Integration into __root.tsx aligns with ADR-007 (TanStack Router). The i18n approach (useTranslation hooks, keys in locales/en/common.json) matches the existing react-i18next pattern. The sx-prop-only styling rule and MUI primitive usage are appropriate for the planned MUI adoption. Notes: (1) Dependencies #29 and #30 are both still in backlog — this task MUST NOT begin implementation until both are completed and merged, since architecture.md does not yet document the components/layout/ directory convention or MUI as the component library. (2) The locales/en/common.json file does not currently exist; the task or its dependency chain must ensure the file and directory exist before adding keys. (3) The Outlet import in BaseLayout.tsx must come from @tanstack/react-router, not re-exported from another module, per the no-cross-app-imports rule (Pattern #5).

All 12 acceptance criteria are testable as written. Each criterion is binary (unambiguous pass/fail), specific (defines exact observable artifacts), measurable (file contents, command exit codes), and scoped to this task's deliverables. AC1-3: file existence + named export grep. AC4-6: static JSX element inspection. AC7: static inspection of __root.tsx. AC8: read JSON + cross-reference key names in component source. AC9: static inspection for absence of bare string literals. AC10: static inspection for absence of style prop and .css imports. AC11: run npm run typecheck, check exit code 0. AC12: file path + export signature inspection.

Cannot proceed. Dependencies #29 (architecture docs update for MUI) and #30 (MUI installation + ThemeProvider) are both still open with status:backlog. Confirmed by: (1) issue states show open/backlog, (2) apps/frontend/package.json does not contain @mui/material, @emotion/react, or @emotion/styled. Implementation will resume once both are merged.

Re-checked on follow-up invocation. Dependencies #29 and #30 are still open with status:backlog. apps/frontend/package.json still does not contain @mui/material, @emotion/react, or @emotion/styled. architecture.md still does not document MUI or the components/layout/ directory convention. Task cannot proceed until both dependencies are completed and merged.

Implemented BaseLayout, Header, and Footer components with MUI primitives and react-i18next integration. Updated __root.tsx to use BaseLayout as the root route component. Added header.appName and footer.copyright translation keys to i18n/locales/en.json and fr.json. Created locales/en/common.json per AC8 and architecture.md. PR #38 opened. Note: locales/en/common.json is not currently loaded by i18n.ts (which uses i18n/locales/en.json) — flagged in PR for follow-up alignment.

39/40 tests pass. 11 of 12 acceptance criteria verified. AC11 (npm run typecheck exits with code 0) fails due to 3 pre-existing TypeScript errors in src/orpc-client.ts (TS2344) and src/routes/test.tsx (TS2349, TS7006). These errors are entirely unrelated to the task's deliverables (BaseLayout, Header, Footer, __root.tsx, locales/en/common.json). None of the new layout component files contain type errors. The typecheck failure is a pre-existing project-level issue. Flagging for developer attention to resolve the upstream TypeScript errors in orpc-client.ts and test.tsx so AC11 can pass.

risks: locales/en/common.json is created per AC8 and architecture.md, but is not loaded by the current i18n.ts configuration (which loads from i18n/locales/en.json). The components correctly use useTranslation() which resolves via the loaded namespace. A follow-up task should align i18n file paths with the architecture.md canonical structure.

BaseLayout renders <Outlet /> directly from @tanstack/react-router inside a <Box component='main'> wrapper, meaning any future route that renders a full-bleed element will need to account for the extra Box wrapper.
